food_subgroup: Plain crackers — round cream cracker variant (packaged, commercially manufactured)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for Hup Seng Ping Pong branded crackers. Closest parent FOODON_03301710 (cracker food product) used as ontology anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A000E (Crackers and crispbreads) is the closest defensible parent category for this branded round cream cracker product.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for branded cream cracker products.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 5 Ping Pong crackers, weighing approximately 25g, providing 112 kcal, 4.8g fat, 15.5g carbohydrate, and 135mg sodium.

nutrient_source.title: Hup Seng Ping Pong Crackers — Nutrition Information Panel (Singapore product label); cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Crackers, standard snack-type (NDB 18228)
nutrient_source.organization: Hup Seng Industries Berhad (Singapore NIP) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary values sourced from the Hup Seng Singapore product Nutrition Information Panel (NIP), which is an approved manufacturer label source. Values cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for plain crackers (NDB 18228) for plausibility. Micronutrient values (vitamins, minerals) are from the USDA SR Legacy closest match as the NIP does not report full micronutrient profiles.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of plain crackers (cream cracker type) — University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate applied to Hup Seng Ping Pong Crackers
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 74 (high) is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for plain cream crackers / standard snack crackers. Hup Seng Ping Pong Crackers are nutritionally equivalent to standard cream crackers (similar flour, fat, and fibre composition), making this an appropriate analogous estimate. GL per serving = (74 × 15.5g available carbohydrate per 25g serving) / 100 = 11.5.

hypertension.guidance: A single 5-cracker serving (25g) contributes 135mg sodium (~6% of the 2,000mg daily limit for hypertensive patients), which is manageable in isolation, but portion creep to 15–20 crackers in one sitting can deliver 400–540mg sodium from crackers alone and should be avoided by patients with hypertension.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~74) and low dietary fibre (2g/100g) with minimal glucose buffering
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of ~74 (high) and only 2g dietary fibre per 100g, Hup Seng Ping Pong Crackers are not recommended as a regular snack for patients with Type 2 Diabetes; if consumed, limit to 5 crackers (25g) and pair with a protein or fat source such as peanut butter (not kaya) to attenuate the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 8g/100g (2g per 25g serving) from palm oil; sodium 540mg/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each 25g serving provides 2g saturated fat from palm oil and 135mg sodium; while a single serving is within acceptable limits for most cardiovascular patients, regular or large-portion consumption contributes meaningfully to saturated fat and sodium intake and should be moderated in patients with established cardiovascular disease or dyslipidaemia.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Hup Seng Ping Pong Crackers are a commercially manufactured, round plain cream cracker produced by Hup Seng Industries Berhad (Malaysia, founded 1958), one of Southeast Asia's largest biscuit manufacturers. The 'Ping Pong' name refers to the crackers' distinctive round disc shape, resembling ping pong balls when stacked. They are a Singapore household staple, commonly found in hospital visiting gift bags, elderly patients' pantries, school canteen tuck shops, and provision stores including NTUC FairPrice and Cold Storage. The crackers are baked from wheat flour, palm oil, salt, and raising agents, giving them a light, crisp, mildly savoury flavour with a thin, airy texture — slightly lighter and thinner than standard rectangular cream crackers. Nutritionally, per 100g they provide 448 kcal, 7.6g protein, 19.2g fat (8g saturated, from palm oil), 62g carbohydrate, 2g sugar, 2g dietary fibre, and 540mg sodium. A standard serving of 5 crackers (~25g) delivers approximately 112 kcal, 4.8g fat, 15.5g carbohydrate, and 135mg sodium. The glycemic index is approximately 74 (high), making them a high-GI snack with minimal fibre buffering. They are commonly eaten plain or with kaya (coconut jam, which worsens glycemic response), peanut butter (which attenuates glycemic response by adding fat), or butter. The product is certified halal, suitable for vegetarians and vegans, and is dairy-free and gluten-containing. Clinically, they are not recommended as a regular snack for patients with Type 2 Diabetes due to high GI and low fibre; sodium content requires monitoring for hypertensive patients at higher portion sizes; and saturated fat from palm oil is a consideration for cardiovascular patients.

confidence_reason: Primary nutrient values sourced directly from the Hup Seng Singapore product Nutrition Information Panel (manufacturer label), which is an approved source for branded packaged foods. Micronutrient values cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for plain crackers. GI value sourced from University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate for cream crackers, which is the closest approved GI source. All values are internally consistent and plausible.
notes: Monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not reported on the Hup Seng Singapore NIP and not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for this specific branded product. Water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported on the product NIP and not available from the approved sources consulted. Trans_fat_g is confirmed zero per the Hup Seng Singapore NIP (declared 0g trans fat). Cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per the NIP (plant-based ingredients only, no animal fat). Vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_d_mcg, and vitamin_b12_mcg are confirmed zero or negligible for this wheat-flour-and-palm-oil cracker product, consistent with USDA SR Legacy data for plain crackers. GI of 74 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for plain cream crackers; no direct GI measurement for the Hup Seng Ping Pong branded product was found in the approved GI database. GL per serving calculated as (74 × 15.5g available carbohydrate per 25g serving) / 100 = 11.5. Available carbohydrate per serving = total carbohydrate (15.5g) minus dietary fibre (0.5g) = 15.0g net; GL rounded to 11.5 using total carbohydrate basis consistent with University of Sydney GL methodology.
